title_en: An-Nawawi and His Comprehensive Supplication Collection
content_en:
Yahya ibn Sharaf Al-Nawawi (631–676 AH / 1233–1277 CE) was one of the greatest Islamic scholars of the medieval period, a towering figure in Shafi'i jurisprudence, hadith commentary, and Islamic spirituality. Born in Nawa (a town near Damascus in Syria), he spent most of his scholarly life in Damascus, where he taught at the Dar al-Hadith al-Ashrafiyyah. His short life — he died at approximately 45 years of age — was remarkable for its productivity, producing works that became permanent references in Islamic scholarship.

Al-Nawawi's major works span multiple disciplines. In hadith, his commentary on Sahih Muslim (al-Minhaj Sharh Sahih Muslim) is the definitive commentary on that collection. His Riyadh as-Salihin is the most widely used hadith anthology for general Islamic education. His works on hadith terminology (al-Irshad, al-Taqrib wa at-Taysir) are among the most taught introductions to the hadith sciences. And his al-Adhkar is the most comprehensive collection of authenticated prophetic supplications and formulas of remembrance.

Al-Adhkar an-Nawawiyyah (The Remembrances of Al-Nawawi), commonly known simply as al-Adhkar, is organized to cover the supplications (adhkar is the plural of dhikr) appropriate for every time, place, and circumstance of Muslim life. From the supplication upon waking to the supplication upon going to sleep, from entering the mosque to leaving it, from beginning a journey to arriving at one's destination, from times of difficulty to times of joy — Al-Nawawi assembled the authenticated prophetic formulas for all of them.

The work represents Al-Nawawi's conviction that authentic dhikr practice — using the specific formulas transmitted from the Prophet with sound chains — is a spiritual discipline as important as any other, and that it requires the same critical methodology as hadith authentication. He explicitly notes the chains and reliability of the supplications he includes, distinguishing the strongly authenticated from those with some weakness in their chains.
title_id: Al-Nawawi dan Koleksi Doanya yang Komprehensif
content_id:
Yahya ibn Syaraf an-Nawawi (631-676 H / 1233-1277 M) adalah salah satu ulama Islam terbesar pada periode abad pertengahan, tokoh yang sangat berpengaruh dalam fikih Syafi'i, komentar hadis, dan spiritualitas Islam. Lahir di Nawa (sebuah kota dekat Damaskus di Suriah), beliau menghabiskan sebagian besar kehidupan ilmiahnya di Damaskus, di mana beliau mengajar di Dar al-Hadits al-Asyrafiyyah. Kehidupannya yang singkat — beliau wafat pada usia sekitar 45 tahun — sangat luar biasa dalam produktivitasnya, menghasilkan karya-karya yang menjadi referensi permanen dalam keilmuan Islam.

Karya-karya utama an-Nawawi mencakup berbagai disiplin ilmu. Dalam bidang hadis, komentarnya atas Sahih Muslim (al-Minhaj Syarh Sahih Muslim) adalah komentar definitif atas koleksi tersebut. Riyadh as-Salihin-nya adalah antologi hadis yang paling banyak digunakan untuk pendidikan Islam umum. Karyanya tentang terminologi hadis (al-Irsyad, al-Taqrib wa at-Taysir) termasuk di antara pengantar ilmu-ilmu hadis yang paling banyak diajarkan. Dan al-Adzkarnya adalah koleksi paling komprehensif dari doa-doa kenabian yang sahih dan formula-formula zikir.

Al-Adzkar an-Nawawiyyah (Zikir-Zikir an-Nawawi), yang umumnya dikenal hanya sebagai al-Adzkar, disusun untuk mencakup doa-doa (adzkar adalah jamak dari dzikir) yang sesuai untuk setiap waktu, tempat, dan keadaan kehidupan Muslim. Dari doa saat bangun tidur hingga doa saat akan tidur, dari memasuki masjid hingga meninggalkannya, dari memulai perjalanan hingga tiba di tujuan, dari masa-masa sulit hingga masa-masa gembira — an-Nawawi mengumpulkan formula-formula kenabian yang sahih untuk semuanya.

Karya ini mencerminkan keyakinan an-Nawawi bahwa praktik zikir yang otentik — menggunakan formula-formula spesifik yang diriwayatkan dari Nabi dengan sanad yang kuat — adalah disiplin spiritual yang sama pentingnya dengan yang lainnya, dan bahwa ia memerlukan metodologi kritis yang sama seperti dalam autentikasi hadis. Beliau secara eksplisit mencatat sanad dan keandalan doa-doa yang beliau sertakan, membedakan yang kuat autentikasinya dari yang memiliki kelemahan dalam sanadnya.
content_ar: Yahya ibn Sharaf النووي (631–676 AH / 1233–1277 CE) was one of the greatest Islamic العلماء of the medieval period, a towering figure in الشافعي jurisprudence, الحديث commentary, and Islamic spirituality. Born in Nawa (a town near Damascus in Syria), he spent most of his العلمي الحياة in Damascus, where he taught at the Dar al-الحديث al-Ashrafiyyah. His short الحياة — he died at approximately 45 years of age — was remarkable for its productivity, producing works that became permanent references in Islamic scholarship. النووي's major works span multiple disciplines. In الحديث, his commentary on صحيح مسلم (al-Minhaj Sharh صحيح مسلم) is the definitive commentary on that collection. His Riyadh as-Salihin is the most widely used الحديث anthology for general Islamic education. His works on الحديث terminology (al-Irshad, al-Taqrib wa at-Taysir) are among the most taught introductions to the الحديث sciences. And his al-Adhkar is the most comprehensive collection of authenticated prophetic supplications and formulas of الذكر. Al-Adhkar an-Nawawiyyah (The Remembrances of النووي), commonly known simply as al-Adhkar, is organized to cover the supplications (adhkar is the plural of الذكر) appropriate for every time, place, and circumstance of مسلم الحياة. From the الدعاء upon waking to the الدعاء upon going to sleep, from entering the المسجد to leaving it, from beginning a journey to arriving at one's destination, from times of difficulty to times of joy — النووي assembled the authenticated prophetic formulأما all of them. The work represents النووي's conviction that authentic الذكر practice — using the specific formulas transmitted from النبي ﷺ with sound chains — is a spiritual discipline as المهم as any other, and that it requires the same البالغ الأهمية methodology as الحديث authentication. He explicitly notes the chains and reliability of the supplications he includes, distinguishing the strongly authenticated from those with some الضعف in their chains.
